title: space_set_update uses flat semantic patch keys, not JSON Pointer paths — correct for this spec
description: Standard RFC 8620 /set uses JSON Pointer paths as patch keys (e.g. 'members/0/nick'). The JMAP Chat spec explicitly departs from this for Space/set updates, using named semantic mutation keys (addMembers, removeMembers, etc.) because the server needs to enforce permissions and ordering on each operation. space_set_update correctly builds a flat map of named keys rather than trying to construct pointer paths. A contributor familiar with RFC 8620 might 'fix' this to use JSON Pointers — that would be wrong. The spec note in the docstring ('Space uses semantic mutation keys') should be considered a mandatory comment, not a cleanup target.
